  • Overview

SEARCH

All Classes and Interfaces

Class
Description
ActionQueue
This class provides the ability to pop items into a queue thread-safely and know they'll happen later.
ActionQueueKiller
This class exists to properly kill off multiple action queues
Body
This class represents the body of an HTML message.
BodyProcessor
This code is responsible for extracting the Body from an HTTP request.
ByteUtils
Handy helpers when working with bytes
ConcurrentSet<T>
This uses a [ConcurrentHashMap] as its base.
Config
If the user has no configuration file, this class contains code that will run to assist them.
Constants
Very important system design decisions are made here.
Context
Holds important system-wide data and methods.
CryptoUtils
Handy helpers for dealing with cryptographic functions
Db<T extends DbData<?>>
This allows us to run some disk-persistence operations more consistently on any data that extends from DbData
DbData<T>
Extend from this class to create a data value that is intended to be stored in the database.
ExtendedExecutor
This lets us capture the stack traces thrown in a thread, which the typical Executor does not.
FileUtils
Helper functions for working with files.
ForbiddenUseException
This is thrown when the user action is prevented by a restriction we put on the system.
FullSystem
This class is responsible for kicking off the entire system.
FunctionalTesting
Tools to enable system-wide integration testing
FunctionalTesting.TestResponse
A Response designed to work with FunctionalTesting
Headers
Details extracted from the headers.
HtmlParseNode
These nodes represent the types of things we may encounter when parsing an HTML string, which for our purposes is just the types in ParseNodeType.
HtmlParser
This class converts HTML strings to HTML object trees.
ILogger
The functions necessary for logging runtime information
InputStreamUtils
Handy helpful utilities for working with input streams.
InvariantException
An exception specific to our invariants.
Invariants
Utilities for asserting invariants within the code.
ISocketWrapper
This is the public interface to ISocketWrapper, whose purpose is to make our lives easier when working with Socket.
Logger
Implementation of ILogger
LoggingActionQueue
This class is very similar to ActionQueue but is focused on Logging.
LoggingContext
Holds important system-wide data and methods for logging
LoggingLevel
An enumeration of the levels of logging our system provides.
LRUCache<T>
A simple Least-Recently Used Cache See LRU
MyThread
This class exists just to avoid needing to handle the exception when I use a regular Thread.sleep()
ParseNodeType
The different kinds of things in an HTML document.
ParsingException
Thrown If a failure takes place during parsing in any of the parsing code of the framework.
RegexUtils
Handy helpers to make regular expression marginally easier / more efficient, etc.
Request
An HTTP request.
Response
Represents an HTTP response.
RunnableWithDescription<E extends Throwable>
This class is to improve maintainability in the system.
SearchUtils
Utilities for searching collections of data
Server
The purpose here is to make it marginally easier to work with a ServerSocket.
SetOfSws
This is a data structure of the live set of ISocketWrapper in our system.
SocketWrapper
This wraps Sockets to make them more particular to our use case
StacktraceUtils
Helper functions for manipulating stack traces.
StartLine
This class holds data and methods for dealing with the "start line" in an HTTP request.
StartLine.PathDetails
Some essential characteristics of the path portion of the start line
StartLine.Verb
These are the HTTP Verbs we handle.
StaticFilesCache
This is a store of data for any static data we are sending to the client.
StatusLine
This class represents the text that is sent back in a Response
StatusLine.StatusCode
See see Status Codes
StopwatchUtils
This class provides some tools for running a virtual stopwatch while code is running, to examine code speed.
StringUtils
Some helper methods for Strings.
TagInfo
Represents a tag.
TagName
Possible tag names per the W3C HTML spec.
TemplateProcessor
In order to perform speedy template rendering, it is necessary to get the template converted into this class.
TemplateRenderException
This exception is thrown when we try to render a string template but fail to include a key for one of the key values - that is, if the template is "hello {foo}", and our map doesn't include a value for foo, this exception will get thrown.
TemplateSection
Represents one item in the list that will eventually be cooked up into a fully-rendered string.
TestFailureException
Thrown when a test fails
TestFramework
These are utility functions for basic automated testing.
TestLogger
This implementation of Logger has a few extra functions that only apply to tests, like TestLogger.test(String)
TheBrig
This class is responsible for monitoring the inmates who have misbehaved in our system.
ThrowingConsumer<T,E extends Throwable>
This exists so that we are able to slightly better manage exceptions in a highly threaded system.
ThrowingRunnable<E extends Throwable>
This exists so that we are able to slightly better manage exceptions in a highly threaded system.
ThrowingSupplier<T,E extends Throwable>
This exists so that we are able to slightly better manage exceptions in a highly threaded system.
TimeUtils
 
UnderInvestigation
Looking for bad actors in our system
WebEngine
This class contains the basic internet capabilities.
WebEngine.HttpVersion
 
WebFramework
Responsible for the logistics after socket connection
